sanskrit:
नात्यश्नतस्तु योगोऽस्ति न चैकान्तमनश्नतः।
न चातिस्वप्नशीलस्य न चाजाग्रतोऽर्जुन।।
transliteration:
nāty-aśnatas tu yogo ’sti na cai-kāntam anaśnataḥ |
na cāti-svapna-śīlasya na cājāgrato ’rjuna ||
literal_meaning: Not for one who eats too much, nor for one who eats too little, nor for one who sleeps too much, nor for one who stays awake too much, O Arjuna, is yoga possible.
contextual_meaning: Optimal self-regulation—neither indulgence nor extreme asceticism—is essential for psychological integration and spiritual practice.
psychological_state: Striving for the golden mean between sensory excess and rigid suppression; the ego oscillates between craving and denial.
trigger_scenario: Binge-scrolling until 3 a.m., then trying to compensate with an overly strict diet and 4 a.m. workouts—ending up exhausted and self-critical.
anchor_text: Balance in food, sleep, and wakefulness steadies the mind for yoga.
